 Open the app [...] # Ben Goodey’s Post

Ben Goodey

 Report this post [...] We had this one client where their name exactly matched the keyword...and this was causing a ton of issues for their GEO/AEO. Imagine the keywords are "writing tool" and the brand is called WritingTool. Despite being a leading software in the space, Google's AI Overviews never recommended them. We noticed that when you searched "WritingTool" Google also tried to autocorrect you. So, we guessed (accurately) that it was confused by the exact match name. To fix it, we started calling them "WritingTool (aka writingtool[.]com)" in a few prominent blogs on their site and created relevant guest posts with the same. Within a couple of weeks, they were #1 in the AI overview and it was calling them "writingtool[.]com". Fringe case. But a great example of where simple Branded GEO can unlock a lot of

results.title: B2B Zero-Click Marketing | Sagefrog
results.content:
ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar tools synthesize information from many sources. GEO success means your brand is trusted enough to be in that synthesis, so the “librarian” proactively recommends you, not just quotes you. Note: 80% of global B2B buyers in the tech industry use GEO as much as traditional search when researching vendors.2

AI-generated overviews now appear in most B2B search queries, often pushing traditional blue links below the fold. To win, you need to get seen (SEO), quoted (AEO), and recommended (GEO).

## The Rise of Agentic Search in B2B Marketing

Instead of manually combing through dozens of websites, buyers now issue complex prompts to generative AI like “find me the top three marketing agencies for SaaS companies with Series B experience.” [...] ## View this Resource

"\" indicates required fields

# B2B Zero-Click Marketing: How to Win When Clicks Disappear

By: Corinne Sinesi

TL;DR

Zero-click marketing is now central to B2B growth as buyers increasingly rely on AI summaries and search results without clicking through. Success requires optimizing for SEO, AEO, and GEO, building entity authority, creating high-information content, measuring mindshare over clicks, and ensuring high-intent visitors convert when they do arrive.

Inside This Guide

In 2026, a visitor to your website is a luxury; a mention in an AI summary is a necessity. [...] SEO ensures your content is indexed and ranking in search results. Think of it as placing your book in the correct section of the library so people can find it.

### Answer Engine Optimization (AEO): The quote on the back of the book.

AEO focuses on featured snippets, voice assistants, and AI quick answers. It’s about structuring content so search engines quote you directly when answering questions.

### Generative Engine Optimization (GEO): The librarian recommends your book.

# How Google AI Mode changes SEO, AEO and AI Search | AirOps & Chris Long
## AirOps | The growth platform for AI search and AEO
1550 subscribers
2 likes

### Description
145 views
Posted: 24 Jun 2026
Chris Long (co-founder, Nectiv) joined us to break down how Google has been intentionally stair-stepping users into AI mode since 2022, and what that means for SEO and content teams right now.

Google's AI mode rollout was never an accident. Every update since SGE has been building toward this.

- Impressions are up. Clicks are not. The old conversion model is broken and needs to be replaced.

0 Citations and brand mentions are different metrics. You need to track both, separately.

- Fan-out queries tell you what AI search is actually looking for. Most teams aren't using this data yet. [...] in line with like the actual SEO AI search efforts that we're doing. So I think that's one of the big ones, right? But I don't think it's as simple as like we're going to get this much organic traffic, it's going to convert at a 1% rate and then you're going to get these many leads. I think that model is like fundamentally broken and and for those of you who don't it's like Kev I think Kevin Indig recently posted this about like all AI search is turning into a reading session. Um that's certainly a really good article too about like kind of like why that this that breaks this model, right? Where it's like people are reading, getting the information they need, and then likely going direct. I we're also encouraging a lot of clients on self-attribution too. So it's like if you have a form, [...] the same thing and maybe a little bit difficult to get ahead. I I would set I would set up some type of monitoring so like what's your content strategy, right? That type of thing. I honestly how you can stay ahead of competitors is like I would find your AI eco I believe AEO is like kind of like four different ecosystems, right? Onsite, affiliate, social, and then some type of reviews, right? Would be the four. I would go and find where those competitors are weak. Like do are the it's like everyone's we talk about onsite a lot, but like, okay, if you've let's say you've established Reddit or so the social sphere is like Reddit, LinkedIn, whatever matters. Well, do your competitors have a presence there, right? Are they know are they like are they talked about that for like the key
